message.content:
<deliberation>
Approach:
- Reviewed each main GDPR node against the entire provided policy chunk.
- Pulled in and considered each node’s 1-hop cross-references where applicable.
- Flagged only explicit differences in constraints/scope as deviations; treated missing/omitted GDPR details as Non-Deviations per instruction 4.
Findings:
- REG-021 (Art13(3)): Policy explicitly prohibits any further processing (“not used for any other purposes”), which is stricter than Art 13(3) that permits further processing with prior information; classified as a Severity deviation (over-compliance). Considered Art13(2) as the referenced requirement.
- REG-032 (Art17(1)): Policy mentions deletion upon consent withdrawal or service termination. No explicit contradiction with Art 17(1) or its references (Art 6(1), Art 21(1), Art 21(2)); omissions about other grounds/timeframe are treated as Non-Deviation.
- REG-005 (Art9(1)): The listed data categories do not include special-category data; “content of the comment” could hypothetically include such data but the policy does not state it will process special categories. No explicit contradiction with Art 9(1) or its exceptions (Art 9(2)); omissions treated as Non-Deviation.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art13(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-020"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Where the controller intends to further process the personal data for a purpose other than that for which the personal data were collected, the controller shall provide the data subject prior to that further processing with information on that other purpose and with any relevant further information as referred to in paragraph 2.",
        "policy_quote": "Your data is not used for any other purposes or shared with third parties.",
        "mismatch": "GDPR allows further processing with prior information, but the policy categorically disallows any other-purpose processing.",
        "analysis": "The policy’s blanket prohibition on further processing is stricter than GDPR Art13(3), which contemplates allowing further processing if prior information (and Art13(2) details) is provided."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-032",
    "clause": "Art17(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-039", "REG-040"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-005",
    "clause": "Art9(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-006"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
